examples.title: Simple Reflex Agent Pseudocode
examples.description: Implements a simple reflex agent using condition-action rules
examples.code:
FUNCTION Simple-Reflex-Agent(percept):
 state ← Interpret-Input(percept)
 rule ← Rule-Match(state, rules)
 action ← rule.ACTION
 RETURN action

# Example: Vacuum Cleaner
RULES:
 IF state = 'Dirty' THEN action = 'Suck'
 IF state = 'Clean' AND location = 'A' THEN action = 'Move Right'
 IF state = 'Clean' AND location = 'B' THEN action = 'Move Left'
examples.explanation: Simple reflex agent ignores percept history and acts only on current percept using condition-action rules. Fast but limited in partially observable environments.

examples.title: Model-Based Reflex Agent Pseudocode
examples.description: Implements a model-based reflex agent with internal state
examples.code:
FUNCTION Model-Based-Agent(percept):
 state ← Update-State(state, action, percept, model)
 rule ← Rule-Match(state, rules)
 action ← rule.ACTION
 RETURN action

# Internal state maintains:
# - World model: how environment evolves
# - How agent's actions affect environment
# State is updated at each step using transition model
examples.explanation: Model-based agent maintains internal state to track unobservable aspects. Uses a model of how the world works to update state and make better decisions.

examples.title: Goal-Based Agent Pseudocode
examples.description: Implements goal-based agent that plans actions to achieve goals
examples.code:
FUNCTION Goal-Based-Agent(percept):
 state ← Update-State(state, action, percept)
 goals ← Determine-Goals(state)
 plan ← Search-Plan(state, goals)
 action ← First(plan)
 RETURN action

# Uses search algorithms:
# - A* Search
# - Hill Climbing
# - Planning algorithms
examples.explanation: Goal-based agent considers future consequences and plans sequences of actions to achieve desired goals. More flexible than reflex agents.
examples.timeComplexity: Depends on search algorithm (exponential to polynomial)

examples.title: Utility-Based Agent Pseudocode
examples.description: Implements utility-based agent that maximizes expected utility
examples.code:
FUNCTION Utility-Based-Agent(percept):
 state ← Update-State(state, action, percept)
 utility ← Evaluate-State(state)
 action ← Maximize-Expected-Utility(state, actions)
 RETURN action

# Utility Function: maps states to real numbers
# Chooses action that maximizes expected utility
# Handles tradeoffs between multiple goals
examples.explanation: Utility-based agent evaluates different outcomes using a utility function and chooses actions that maximize expected utility, handling tradeoffs between competing goals.

examples.title: PEAS Specification for Taxi Agent
examples.description: Complete PEAS specification for an autonomous taxi
examples.code:
# PEAS for Autonomous Taxi Driver

# PERFORMANCE MEASURE:
- Safe driving (minimize accidents)
- Reach destination quickly
- Follow traffic laws
- Maximize passenger comfort
- Maximize profit

# ENVIRONMENT:
- Roads, traffic, pedestrians
- Weather conditions
- Passengers
- Other vehicles
- Traffic signals

# ACTUATORS:
- Steering wheel
- Accelerator/brake pedals
- Horn
- Turn signals
- Display (navigation)

# SENSORS:
- Cameras
- GPS
- Speedometer
- Radar/LIDAR
- Touch sensors (passenger input)
examples.explanation: This demonstrates complete PEAS specification for a complex real-world agent. The taxi environment is partially observable (can't see everything), stochastic (traffic unpredictable), dynamic, continuous, and multi-agent.
